_description: Architecture Decision Records — maintained by Architect-Agent and DevOps-Agent

decisions.title: Dual database for 152-FZ compliance
decisions.context: Beauty CRM handles PII (client names, phones, medical data for Scenario B). Russian 152-FZ requires PII stored on Russian servers.
decisions.decision: Supabase PostgreSQL for non-PII (bookings, schedules, services). Timeweb Cloud PostgreSQL for PII (client profiles, medical records).
decisions.tradeoffs: Increased complexity in joins across DBs, need sync layer. But ensures legal compliance and Supabase performance for high-traffic reads.

decisions.title: Web pages as primary booking channel
decisions.context: Need to decide between Telegram bot or web for primary booking flow.
decisions.decision: Web pages (Next.js) as primary. Telegram as secondary for notifications only.
decisions.tradeoffs: Web has wider reach and better SEO. Telegram limited to existing users. Web requires more frontend work.

decisions.title: Agent Hub as orchestration backend
decisions.context: Need centralized management for 16 AI agents with different LLM providers.
decisions.decision: Custom Agent Hub REST API on localhost:3000 with Mission Control as management UI.
decisions.tradeoffs: Self-hosted complexity, but full control over agent routing, cost tracking, and prompt management.

decisions.title: Vercel AI SDK v5 for multi-provider abstraction
decisions.context: SingularityBridge needs to support OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google LLMs with unified interface.
decisions.decision: Use Vercel AI SDK v5 as abstraction layer. Supports streaming, function calling, and token tracking across all providers.
decisions.tradeoffs: SDK dependency, but eliminates 3 separate client implementations. Unified streaming/SSE pattern.

decisions.title: Hybrid state management Zustand + MobX State Tree
decisions.context: SB frontend started with MobX State Tree for entity management. New features (chat, audio, screen share) use Zustand.
decisions.decision: Zustand for all new stores (12 stores). MobX retained for legacy entity CRUD (assistant, company, user, team). Plan: migrate MobX to Zustand.
decisions.tradeoffs: Two state paradigms increase cognitive load. But avoids risky full rewrite during active development.

decisions.title: Stateless execution endpoint separate from session chat
decisions.context: External API consumers need one-shot agent execution without session overhead. Internal chat needs context history.
decisions.decision: POST /assistant/:id/execute for stateless. POST /assistant/user-input for session-based with SSE streaming.
decisions.tradeoffs: Two code paths to maintain, but clear separation of concerns. API keys only on stateless path.
alternatives_rejected: Single endpoint with optional session (confusing API)

decisions.title: Company-based multi-tenancy via query filter
decisions.context: SB serves multiple companies. Need data isolation.
decisions.decision: All MongoDB queries filtered by companyId extracted from JWT/API key. No row-level security in DB.
decisions.tradeoffs: Simpler implementation but relies on application layer for isolation. A missed filter = data leak. Consider Mongoose middleware for enforcement.

decisions.title: Socket.io + Pusher dual real-time strategy
decisions.context: Need real-time updates for chat streaming and workspace sync. Direct WebSocket unreliable behind some proxies.
decisions.decision: Socket.io for direct server-client. Pusher as cloud fallback for reliability. Events mirrored to both channels.
decisions.tradeoffs: Double infrastructure cost, but ensures delivery. Pusher handles reconnection and presence tracking.

decisions.title: JWT dual-token схема (15min access + 7d refresh) зафиксирована для Beauty CRM
decisions.context: В s3-architect и s3.5-cyber зафиксирована конкретная конфигурация токенов: access token TTL 15 минут, refresh token TTL 7 дней, multi-tenant контекст. Это решение стало входным контекстом для всех последующих агентов и определило реализацию logout на фронтенде.
decisions.decision: Записать в `knowledge-base/tech-decisions.json`: ключ `auth.jwt.accessTTL: 900`, `auth.jwt.refreshTTL: 604800`, `auth.multiTenant: true`. Это предотвратит переопределение параметров в будущих задачах и конфликты при ревью.
decisions.tradeoffs: Архитектурное решение принято на уровне Phase 2, зафиксировано в threat model документе `docs/compliance/threat-model-auth-phase2.md`.

decisions.title: Явная пометка [ASSUMED] в выводе агента как сигнал неполного брифа
decisions.context: s4-frontend добавил секцию `[ASSUMED]` в свой вывод, указав что компонент чисто фронтендовый. Это означает, что входной бриф не содержал явного утверждения об отсутствии серверной логики, и агент был вынужден делать предположения самостоятельно.
decisions.decision: В Task Brief для frontend-agent добавлять явный раздел: `COMPONENT TYPE: pure-ui | connected | hybrid`. Это устраняет необходимость в предположениях и снижает риск того, что агент случайно добавит серверную логику или пропустит необходимую интеграцию. Шаблон брифа обновить в `knowledge-base/tech-decisions.json`.
decisions.tradeoffs: Task Brief не содержал явного поля CONSTRAINTS с указанием "no API calls, no server state, pure UI component". Агент компенсировал это через assumption-маркировку.

decisions.title: Upfront constraint specification (Tailwind + Lucide + animation type) eliminates ambiguity and drives perfect scores
decisions.context: The task brief explicitly named the UI library (Tailwind), icon library (Lucide), the exact status enum values (pending/running/completed/failed/paused), and the specific animation behavior (pulse for running). The frontend-agent produced a 10/10 output on the first attempt with zero clarification requests.
decisions.decision: For all future UI component tasks, the task brief MUST include: (1) exact status/state enum values, (2) named animation type per state, (3) color semantic intent (e.g., "red = failed, not orange"), (4) icon library name. Encode this as a required checklist in the frontend-agent task brief template to prevent specificity score drops.
decisions.tradeoffs: N/A — success pattern. Contrast with typical frontend tasks that score 6–7 on specificity because the agent must assume color semantics, icon choices, and animation style.

decisions.title: Assumption Marking Convention (`[ASSUMED]`) as Graceful Degradation Signal
decisions.context: Both s4-backend and s4-frontend independently adopted an `[ASSUMED]` annotation convention when operating without upstream architectural input. This allowed them to produce complete, high-quality outputs (both 10/10) while transparently communicating their dependency gaps to downstream agents and the Orchestrator.
decisions.decision: Formalize `[ASSUMED]` as an official agent protocol. Any agent that proceeds without a required upstream input must: (1) prefix affected decisions with `[ASSUMED]`, (2) list all assumptions in a dedicated "Assumptions" section at the top of output, (3) flag to Orchestrator that a reconciliation pass is needed once the missing input arrives. Add this to agent prompt Layer 1 guidelines.
decisions.tradeoffs: N/A — this is a positive emergent behavior.
